reasoning: The resolution date is July 1, 2025, leaving only 45 days from the current date (May 16, 2025). Although Starlink received a license in August 2024 and TRCSL officials previously projected an April 2025 launch, recent reporting from mid-May 2025 reveals a complete stall in the rollout. The Sri Lankan government (specifically the Defense Ministry) placed the launch on hold in early May to demand a mechanism for lawful data interception for national security purposes and is now seeking to revise the original license terms. Establishing such capabilities (likely requiring local ground gateways and legal agreements) and navigating Sri Lanka's bureaucracy typically takes many months, akin to similar telecommunications disputes in the region. Security analysts as of May 15 reported 'no immediate resolution in sight' to this standoff. Given the short 45-day window until July 1 and the severity of these new regulatory and security roadblocks, it is very unlikely Starlink will be officially marked 'Available' in Sri Lanka before the cutoff.
